Title: Procurement Officer
Description:
About the role

We are seeking an experienced Procurement Officer to join our dynamic team at PT. OCS Global Services (OCS Indonesia) in South Jakarta. As a Procurement Officer, you will play a crucial role in supporting our global supply chain operations and ensuring the timely procurement of goods and services to meet the needs of our business. This is a full-time position, offering the opportunity to work with a talented and collaborative team in our central Jakarta office.
What you'll be doing
Manage the end-to-end procurement process, including sourcing, negotiating, and contracting with vendors
Conduct market research to identify new suppliers and negotiate favourable pricing and terms
Monitor and maintain inventory levels, ensuring timely replenishment and optimal stock levels
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business requirements and source appropriate products and services
Implement and maintain efficient procurement procedures and policies
Analyse procurement data and provide recommendations to improve processes and reduce costs
Stay up-to-date with market trends, regulations, and best practices in the procurement field
What we're looking for
Minimum 2 - 3 years of experience in a procurement or supply chain role in any Industry, preferably experience handling procurement in Facility Services.
Strong negotiation and vendor management skills with the ability to build and maintain relationships with suppliers
Proficiency in data analysis and the use of procurement software and tools
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams
Ability to work under pressure, prioritise tasks, and meet deadlines
Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, or a related field
What we offer

At PT. OCS Global Services (OCS Indonesia), we are committed to providing a supportive and rewarding work environment for our employees. In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a range of benefits, including:
Comprehensive health insurance coverage
Opportunities for professional development and career growth
Flexible work arrangements to maintain a healthy work-life balance
Team-building activities and social events to foster a collaborative culture

About us

PT. OCS Global Services (OCS Indonesia) is a leading provider of integrated supply chain solutions in the Manufaktur, Transportasi & Logistik industry. With a strong presence across Indonesia, we are committed to delivering excellence to our customers through innovative technologies, sustainable practices, and a talented team of professionals. Join us in shaping the future of procurement and logistics in Indonesia.
Apply now to become our next Procurement Officer and be part of our dynamic team!

a. Ask/receive order confirmation from suppliers

a. Incoming shipment discrepancy on Qty or materials
b. Work with forwarder or WH on shipment isolation
c. Follow up with supplier on shipment discrepancy and insurance company for claim on damaged
F. Logistics support on Quality management
a. Instruct forwarder to sort and isolate affected products
b. Arrange with plants for repair and/or return shipment
c. Follow up until issue is resolved and closed

a. Monthly reconciliation between SAP against warehouse and other sites records
b. Investigate and work with relevant team to revolse any discrepancy

-At least 2-year experience in logistics, supply chain, or purchasing

D. Basic knowledge in warehouse operation
E. Intermediate or higher level in using Excel software
F. Good process understanding of inbound from oversea supplier to destination and outbound from either our warehouse or supplier (in case of drop shipment) to customer destination A. Experience in working with forwarder and warehouse.
B. Hands-on and fluent in English. Able to speak Chinese will be added advantage

Title: Customer Service and Operation Specialist (Airfreight)
Description:
Position Summary
We are looking for a dedicated and detail-oriented freight Customer Service Senior to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will possess a strong background in freight forwarding and a passion for delivering exceptional customer service. This role involves managing customer inquiries, coordinating and handling airfreight (Main focus) and oceanfreight shipments from end to end, and ensuring timely and accurate communication with clients and internal teams.
 Customer Support

- Address customer questions and issues promptly, ensuring high levels of satisfaction.
Shipment Coordination

Problem Solving

Documentation and Compliance

Collaboration

- Assist in the development of operational improvements to enhance service delivery.
Performance Tracking
- Monitor customer feedback and service metrics, contributing to the continuous improvement of processes and procedures.
  Qualifications
- Degree in Logistics, or a related field preferred.
- Minimum of 3 years of experience in air freight forwarding export and import operation. Lastest working experience is freight forwarder.
- Strong master of air freight forwarding operations, documentation handling, regulations, and industry practices. 
- Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with a strong customer service orientation.
- Detail-oriented with strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
- Proficiency in freight forwarding software (specifically Cargowise) and Microsoft Office Suite.
 What We Offer
- Competitive salary and promotion package.
- Opportunities for rapid business development and career growth.
- A collaborative and supportive work environment.

Title: QC Analyst / Laboratorium
Description:
Job Description:
Conduct Sampling & Routine Testing: Perform sampling of materials (incoming, in-process & finished products); conduct various analytical tests and assays using established methods and instrumentation.
Quality Inspection: Inspect and evaluate the physical and chemical attributes of quality control samples to determine compliance with established quality standards and specifications.
Instrumentation and Equipment: Operate and maintain laboratory equipment, including HPLC and other analytical instruments, ensuring their calibration and optimal working conditions.
Troubleshooting: Identify and resolve technical issues that may arise during testing, working collaboratively with the team to ensure accurate results.
Data Analysis: Accurately record, analyze, and interpret test results, ensuring data integrity and consistency throughout the testing process.
Method Validation: Participate in the validation of analytical methods to ensure the accuracy, precision, and reliability of testing procedures.
Documentation: Maintain comprehensive and organized records of test methods, procedures, results, and any deviations observed during testing, in accordance with applicable procedures and guidelines. Endure timely testing and integrity of data and documentation for product release
Compliance: Adhere to cGMP (current Good Manufacturing Practices) and applicable regulatory guidelines while conducting testing and maintaining laboratory practices, including maintaining inventory of QC retention & stability samples.
Safety: Adhere to all safety protocols and guidelines while working with hazardous chemicals, reagents, and equipment.
Continuous Improvement: Contribute to the enhancement of good laboratory processes, procedures, and practices to optimize efficiency and effectiveness.
Requirements & Qualifications:
Degree / Diploma in Chemistry/Chemical Sciences, Food Technologies/ Food Science or related field.
Minimum 3 years of working in a Food Quality Control Laboratory.
Proficiency in analytical techniques such as physical and/or chemical techniques.
Familiarity with food industry standards (e.g., CPOB, FSSC 22000, ISO 9001, Halal, Kosher, Physical & Chemical Inspection) and cGMP requirements.
Strong attention to detail and accuracy in recording and interpreting data. 
Good communication skills and ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams. 
Problem-solving skills to troubleshoot issues during testing and analysis. 
Knowledge or experience in QC laboratory software and data analysis tools. 
Bilingual in Bahasa Indonesia and English.
Computer literate.
Willing to work in shifts.
Willing to be placed in Romokalisari, Surabaya.

Title: Human Resources Manager25146587
Description:
JOB SUMMARY
As a member of the property Human Resources support staff, he/she works with Human Resources employees to carry out the daily activities of the Human Resource Office including oversight of recruitment, total compensation, and training and development. Additionally, he/she focuses on delivering HR services that meet or exceed the needs of employees and enable business success; as well as ensures compliance with all applicable laws, regulations and operating procedures.
CANDIDATE PROFILE 
Education and Experience
• High school diploma or GED; 3 years experience in the human resources, management operations, or related professional area.
OR
• 2-year degree from an accredited university in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related major; 1 year experience in the human resources, management operations, or related professional area.
CORE WORK ACTIVITIES
Managing Recruitment and Hiring Process
• Assists in the interviewing and hiring of Human Resource employee team members with the appropriate skills, as needed.
• Establishes and maintains contact with external recruitment sources.
• Attends job fairs and ensures documentation of outreach efforts in accordance with Human Resource Standard Operating Procedures.
• Networks with local organizations (e.g., Hotel Association and peers) to source candidates for current or future openings.
• Oversees/monitors candidate identification and selection process.
• Provides subject matter expertise to property managers regarding selection procedures.
• Partners with vendor partners to ensure effective advertisement efforts are being utilized for open positions in appropriate venues to attract a diverse candidate pool.
• Performs quality control on candidate identification/selection.
Administering and Educating Employee Benefits
• Works with the unemployment services provider to respond to unemployment claims; reviews provider reports for accuracy and corrects errors.
• Prepares, audits and distributes unemployment claim activity reports to property management.
• Attends unemployment hearings and ensures property is properly represented.
• Ensures that department has the available resources on hand to administer employee.
Managing Employee Development
• Supports a departmental orientation program for employees to receive the appropriate new hire training to successfully perform their job.
• Ensures employees are cross-trained to support successful daily operations.
• Uses all available on the job training tools for employees; supervise on-going training initiatives and conducts training, when appropriate.
• Ensures coordination and facilitation of new hire orientation program to generate a positive first impression for employees and emphasize the importance of guest service in company culture.
• Ensures attendance by all new hires and participation of the leadership team in training programs
• Collaborates with management team to ensure departmental orientation processes are in place and employees receive the appropriate new hire training to successfully perform their job.
Maintaining Employee Relations
• Assists in maintaining effective employee communication channels in the property (e.g., develops daily communications and assists with regularly scheduled property-wide meetings).
• Reviews progressive discipline documentation for accuracy and consistency, and checks for supportive documentation and is accountable for determining appropriate action.
• Utilizes an “open door” policy to acknowledge employee problems or concerns in a timely manner
• Ensures employee issues are referred to the Department Manager for resolution or escalated to the Director of Human Resources/Multi-Property Director of Human Resources.
• Partners with Loss Prevention to conduct employee accident investigations, as necessary.
• Communicates performance expectations in accordance with job descriptions for each position.
Managing Legal and Compliance Practices
• Ensures employee files contain required employment paperwork, proper performance management and compensation documentation, are properly maintained and secured for the required length of time.
• Ensures compliance with procedure for accessing, reviewing, and auditing employee files and ensure compliance with the Privacy Act.
• Ensures medical records are maintained in a separate, secure and confidential medical file.
• Facilitates random, reasonable belief and post accident drug testing process (in properties where applicable).
• Communicates property rules and regulations via the employee handbook.
• Ensures all safety and security policies (e.g., property removal, lost and found items, blood borne pathogens, accident reporting, and hygiene) are communicated to employees on a regular basis through orientation, property meetings, bulletin boards, etc.
• Conducts periodic claims reviews with Regional Claims office to ensure claims are closed in a timely manner and reserve levels are appropriate for open claims.
• Represents Human Resources at the property Safety Committee; helps to identify ways to create awareness of the importance of safety in the workplace and decrease accident frequency and severity.
• Manages Workers Compensation claims to ensure appropriate employee care and manage costs.
• Oversees the selection/non-selection and offers processes to ensure proper procedures are followed (e.g., valid reasons for selection/non-selection and applicants receive status notifications).

Title: Smelter Operation Readiness - Operator Electrorefinery (ASM & Circulation)
Description:
The purpose of this position:
Rresponsible for the efficient operation and maintenance of the Anode Stripping Machine (ASM) and associated circulation systems. This role ensures the smooth functioning of various processes including filter press, polishing filter, acid purification unit, cooling tower, gas scrubber, and courier analyzer operations.
The key duties and responsibilities of this position:
Begin the shift by checking the operational status of all relevant equipment.
Prepare reagent additives according to operational requirements.
Operate the Anode Stripping Machine to ensure optimal performance.
Run the filter press and polishing filter to maintain effective filtration.
Manage the Acid Purification Unit and ensure purification procedures are followed correctly.
Operate courier analysers to monitor and analyse process parameters.
Monitor system performance, including circulation systems, cooling towers, and gas scrubbers.
Ensure proper flow and distribution of materials in the circulation systems.
Oversee environmental and safety compliance through the operation of cooling towers and gas scrubbers.
Conduct regular inspections and preventive maintenance to avoid equipment downtime.
Perform troubleshooting and corrective actions to maintain continuous operation.
Collect accurate operational data and report findings for process optimization.
End the shift by documenting any issues, maintenance actions, and coordinating with relevant teams for follow-up.
The key challenges in this position:
Maintenance and Preventive Measures:
Ensuring proper maintenance and implementing preventive measures is crucial to avoid machinery breakdowns, which can disrupt production schedules and result in costly repairs.
Operator Training and Skill Development:
Insufficient training and skill gaps can lead to inefficiencies and safety risks. Continuous training programs are essential to keep operators updated on equipment operation, maintenance procedures, and safety protocols.
Compliance with Safety Protocols:
Maintaining a safe work environment is critical. Operators must adhere to safety protocols to prevent accidents, injuries, and equipment damage. Regular safety inspections and risk assessments are necessary.
Downtime and Production Interruptions:
Unplanned downtime due to equipment breakdowns or maintenance activities can significantly impact productivity. Implementing a robust maintenance program and having readily available spare parts can help minimize downtime.
Environmental and Safety Standards:
Managing operations such as cooling towers and gas scrubbers requires strict adherence to environmental and safety standards to ensure compliance and prevent any hazardous incidents.
Data Management and Analysis:
Accurate data collection and analysis are essential for process optimization. Operators need to ensure that data from courier analyzers and other monitoring systems is correctly recorded and analyzed.
The background, education, and work experience needed to succeed in this job are:
High School with minimum 6 years experience in chemical, metal, or mineral industry. Or Diploma with minimum 4 years experience in chemical, metal, or mineral industry. Or Bachelor with minimum 2 years experience in chemical, metal, or mineral industry.
Experience in operating industrial machinery, particularly in a production or manufacturing environment.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality.
Good communication and teamwork skills.


Core Competencies (Knowledge, Skills & Characters)
Knowledge of pump operations, diaphragm, centrifugal, and rotary pumps
Knowledge of fluid and electrolyte circulation includes pipeline connection, type of valves (ball, control, check, gate, globe, butterfly), and heat exchangers.
Strong understanding of safety protocols and regulations to prevent accidents and ensure a safe working environment.
Ability to identify and address operational issues quickly and effectively.
Meticulous in monitoring equipment performance and conducting maintenance.
Ability to work collaboratively with other operators and departments.
Deadline : September, 22nd 2025

Title: Production Planning Manager (Toys/Garment/Footwear)
Description:
Job description
We are a British-owned company specializing in toy manufacturing across Asia, and we are seeking an experienced Production Planning Manager based in Indonesia. In this role, you will be responsible for managing the entire production planning process to ensure that production schedules align with global demand forecasts and that products are delivered on time. This position requires close collaboration with the Country Manager, suppliers, and internal teams to optimize resource allocation, maintain inventory levels, and improve production efficiency while meeting quality standards.
Working time: Monday to Friday
Location: Subang, with occasional travel required

Responsibilities
1. Functional Responsibilities:
● Collaborate with the Country Manager to ensure production plans align with the company’s
strategic objectives.
● Work closely with suppliers, production teams, and the logistics department to ensure efficient
production processes.
● Liaise with the design and procurement teams to ensure raw materials and components are
available on time.
● Communicate with the global supply chain and logistics teams to coordinate product deliveries.
2. Production Scheduling:
● Develop and implement production schedules based on global demand forecasts and sales
projections.
● Monitor production schedules to ensure they are being followed and adjust plans as needed to
address any delays or issues.
● Coordinate with suppliers and internal teams to ensure timely availability of raw materials and
components.
3. Resource Optimization:
● Optimize the allocation of resources (labor, materials, and equipment) to ensure production
efficiency and minimize downtime.
● Analyze production capacity and identify bottlenecks to improve workflow and efficiency.
4. Inventory Management:
● Work closely with procurement to maintain optimal inventory levels, ensuring that materials are
available without overstocking.
● Monitor inventory levels for both raw materials and finished goods to ensure efficient supply chain
operations.
● Implement strategies to reduce excess inventory while ensuring production continuity.
5. Demand Forecasting & Alignment:
● Collaborate with the sales and marketing teams to understand demand forecasts and adjust
production plans accordingly.
● Ensure that production plans are aligned with global sales and distribution requirements.
6. Cross-Functional Collaboration:
● Work closely with the Quality Assurance, Procurement, and Logistics teams to ensure the smooth
flow of goods through the production process.
● Communicate regularly with the Country Manager and other key stakeholders to ensure
alignment on production priorities and goals.
7. Risk Management:
● Identify potential risks to the production schedule, such as delays in raw material delivery or
equipment breakdowns, and implement mitigation strategies.
● Monitor production performance and resolve any issues that may impact production timelines or
product quality.
8. Reporting & Continuous Improvement:
● Prepare regular reports on production progress, performance metrics, and any issues impacting
production timelines.
● Implement continuous improvement initiatives to enhance production efficiency, reduce costs, and
improve delivery times.

Requirements
● Minimum 10 years of experience in Merchandising (MD) or Production Planning, preferably in the
toy or textile industry.
● Strong knowledge of production planning and scheduling processes.
● Experience with supply chain management and inventory control.
● Proficiency in production planning software and tools.
● Excellent communication and problem-solving skills.
● Ability to manage multiple projects and meet tight deadlines.
● Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

Why you will love working here
● Attractive salary
● International environment
● Regular Performance Reviews and Professional Training

Title: Head of Commercial - Fashion Retail
Description:
About the Company
Our client is the company behind Indonesia’s most recognizable lifestyle bag brands, established in 2009. Operating through a robust omnichannel ecosystem, it integrates offline retail stores, online direct-to-consumer platforms, marketplaces, and consignment channels to ensure broad market reach. With its headquarters in Bandung and a growing national footprint, it continues to expand its presence and influence in Indonesia’s fashion and accessories market.
Role Overview
It is seeking a strategic and commercially driven Head of Commercial to lead the sales and channel performance of its renowned brands. This senior leadership role will oversee both offline and online sales operations, including domestic and international markets, and will play a pivotal role in driving revenue growth, brand expansion, and omnichannel excellence.
You will be responsible for developing strategies that align these teams toward a unified commercial goal while ensuring operational efficiency, channel coordination, and market responsiveness. This includes market assessment, retail planning, and cross-border coordination to establish a strong regional footprint.
Key Responsibilities
Lead Omnichannel Commercial Strategy: Formulate and implement integrated commercial strategies across offline and online channels, ensuring alignment with brand goals and revenue targets.
Oversee Sales Operations (Offline & Online): Manage and drive performance for both offline retail (including stores in Sumatra and Balikpapan) and online channels (Shopee SG, MY, PH), optimizing for growth and profitability.
Retail Expansion: Lead planning and execution for the company’s offline store expansion in Southeast Asia, including market analysis, rollout strategy, and operational readiness.
Team Leadership & Development: Directly oversee and guide two business leads: one for offline retail and one for digital commerce. Provide clear direction, performance management, and professional development support.
Channel Optimization & Coordination: Ensure cross-channel integration, smooth coordination between digital and offline operations, and unified customer experience across all touchpoints.
Sales Performance Management: Monitor and analyze sales performance by channel, location, and campaign. Identify trends and adjust strategies to maximize revenue and conversion.
Pricing & Promotions Strategy: Develop adaptive, data-driven pricing and promotional strategies to drive sales, protect margins, and respond to market conditions.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with marketing, supply chain, and product teams to ensure that commercial initiatives align with brand strategy, product availability, and customer needs.
Marketplace & Partner Management: Build and maintain strong relationships with strategic partners and marketplace platforms, ensuring smooth operations, visibility, and commercial success.
Innovation & Business Development: Identify new revenue streams, digital initiatives, or business model enhancements (e.g., loyalty programs, bundling, or region-specific offers) to strengthen the company’s competitive position.
Candidate Requirements
Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Management, or any related field.
Experience:
Minimum 2 years of experience in a commercial leadership role.
Proven experience in managing both offline retail and online sales channels, including marketplace operations (e.g., Shopee) and direct-to-consumer platforms.
Hands-on experience in leading teams across multiple business units, including regional or multi-location retail operations.
Experience with cross-border e-commerce or Southeast Asia market expansion is a strong advantage.
Business-level proficiency in English, both spoken and written.
Professional Skills:
Strong strategic and analytical thinking, with the ability to interpret sales data and translate insights into action.
Excellent leadership and team development skills; capable of guiding and motivating cross-functional teams.
Deep understanding of omnichannel customer behavior, channel integration, and campaign execution.
Strong negotiation and relationship management skills, particularly in working with marketplace platforms, retail partners, and distributors.
Results-oriented, organized, and adaptable to changing market demands and fast-paced environments.
Why Join Us?
Help grow a mission-driven brand loved by Indonesian families
Lead national expansion with a high-impact leadership role
Join a young, dynamic team passionate about parenting innovation
Key Selling Points
Lead company’s expansion roadmap
Oversee both offline and online sales operations, including domestic and international markets
Be part of credible and renowned brands

Title: Quality Control Project – Store Development

This role combines civil engineering knowledge with quantity surveing, requiring you to review RAB (Rencana Anggaran Biaya), verify area/volume calculations, and conduct on-site review to ensure that what has been planned matches the actual execution in the store.
This is a critical role for someone who is detail-oriented, analytical, and has a sharp eye for both numbers and construction details.
‍
In this role, you will get to :‍
Review Rencana Anggaran Biaya (RAB) for store development projects.

Conduct on-site review to ensure actual construction and procurement match approved plans.
Review the purchase and installation of store development items (fixtures, shelves, AC, lighting, etc.).
Identify and report any discrepancies between the budget plan vs. actual implementation as a findings.
Collaborate with General Procurement Department & Store Development Department to ensure compliance with standards, quality, and cost efficiency
Conduct site measurements, cost analysis, and feasibility studies.
Ensure compliance with local building codes, safety standards, and company policies
‍

Excellent negotiation, analytical, and communication skills.
Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects under tight deadlines
Able to work independently and as part of a team.

Title: Education Counsellor
Description:
About IDP 
IDP is a global leader in international education services that helps people study abroad.
As a key voice in the industry, IDP partners with educational institutions to help them attract the right students and co-owns IELTS, a leading English proficiency test.
Established in 1969 in Australia, IDP has teams based in more than 60 countries. Despite its evolution, IDP’s purpose has remained constant: to help people transform their lives through international education. For more information, visit www.idp.com.
About Student Placement 
At IDP, it’s our goal to help every student fulfil their global potential by streamlining the pathway to studying abroad.
To do this, it takes scale. Each year, almost 100,000 IDP students are studying at more than 800 universities around the world, supported by our local teams who know each country’s education landscape inside and out.
Our unbiased guidance helps students build personalised pathways to success. IDP’s 2,200 counsellors reinforce their own expertise with data-driven insights to give advice that’s reliable and valuable.
For universities, governments and sector bodies, our quality insights and tailored solutions make it easier to reach ambitious international students that will shape the future of campuses worldwide.
About IELTS 
IDP is a proud co-owner of IELTS, the world’s best and most trusted English language test for study, work and migration. For more than 35 years, IELTS has been a pioneering force in the industry, and we continue to lead the sector today.
We believe in using technology to enhance human connection, rather than replace it. We want to empower our test takers to achieve their best score. We offer a choice of paper, computer or online testing, and the opportunity to retake just one skill if a test taker did not achieve their desired score, setting us apart from other high-stakes tests.
With an IELTS score, test takers can showcase their language proficiency and abilities and access more than 12,500 organisations worldwide – from education institutions and employers to government and professional bodies.
IDP ensures widespread accessibility by providing IELTS in more than 2,000 test locations in more than 60 countries.
Role purpose
The Education Counsellor will provide excellent counselling services to guide international students to find the institution that best meets their learning and career aspirations, including providing students with the right information and advice on United Kingdom studies opportunities and local education system, culture and geographic region, assisting with application documentation, facilitating enrolments, and promoting IDP products and services.
Key accountabilities
Drive achievement of your pipeline goals, customer satisfaction, and advocacy targets through each stage of the student journey, including promoting student essential products and services, managing and maintaining leads and successful applications, seeking referral opportunities, encouraging and participating in event and lead generating activity, database maintenance.
Provide outstanding customer service to students, their families and education providers throughout the process, including participating in planning activities to build student engagement, providing students with the right information about admissions, processes and qualification options in a way they can understand easily, and proactively engaging with students and education providers regularly.
Required experience
Experience in a sales or customer service driven environment, including achieving individual and team based targets.
Good understanding of the education sector and placement of students into VET and universities, including immigration requirements and processes.
A Bachelor's Degree, Master's Degree / Post Graduate Degree, any field, United Kingdom Graduate and/or prior experience working as an Education Counsellor or similar role is preferred.
Outstanding stakeholder management and communication skills, including fluency in English, both verbal and written.
Willing to work from our Bandung office - Jl Gardujati No 75.

Title: Medical Representative (Semarang)
Description:
GSK’s portfolio spans three product areas: vaccines, specialty and general medicines and our focus is to deliver better and faster for patients globally. Our unrivalled vaccine portfolio targets infectious diseases at every stage of life, helping to reduce the burden of disease for hundreds of millions of people. GSK has exceptional capabilities in vaccine science and technologies, including adjuvant/protein and mRNA. In specialty medicines, we are at the forefront of HIV prevention and treatment, and we are building our presence in key therapeutic areas such as oncology and immuno-inflammation. General medicines include our inhaled medicines for asthma and COPD, antibiotics and medicines for skin diseases.
By 2031, we aim to deliver more than £33 billion in annual sales – a step-change in performance and growth which will significantly increase the positive impact we can have on the health of billions of patients around the world. And we’re confident in our future. Our pipeline of 21 vaccines and 43 medicines includes many with potential to be first or best-in-class opportunities for patients
We are currently looking for an experienced Medical Representative (Vaccine) who is keen to provide expert medical support and contribute to strategic business planning as one of the clinical representatives.
Key Responsibilities:
Continuously builds knowledge of GSK multi-channel brand strategies and leverages that knowledge with customers in informing on prescribing behaviours
Understands GSK and competitor’s brands features and benefits and applies knowledge effectively in their PFSS and multi-channel selling approach
Uses multiple data sources to analyse and review territory performance dynamics (including competitor performance) identifying territory trends and opportunities for GSK
Builds territory insights that capture opportunities and drive decision making, including the selection and prioritization of customer targets and the development of robust territory coverage call plans
Develop, based on customer insights, multi-channel business plans with strategically aligned ASMART objectives, strategies and tactical activities that deliver business objectives
Effectively engages with customers through a PFSS selling approach or multi-channel selling approach and is proficient in using appropriate multi-channel selling platforms aligned to customer preferences
Why you?
We look for people who appreciate the importance of safety and quality and strive to continuously improve our performance and practices.  We work in an open and transparent environment so you’ll be someone who treats others with respect and integrity – as you will be treated yourself. 
Ideally, we are looking for someone with:
Bachelor degree, preferably within life sciences, with Good Technical & Professional Knowledge
Minimum 3 years professional experience with the same position in pharmaceutical industry
Good communication  and selling skills
Scientific knowledge and patient focused
Strong Interpersonal skill, Proactive and Teamwork (Business Partnership)
Why GSK?
GSK’s dedication to the wellbeing of our employees is reflected in the benefits we offer; including healthcare for yourself and dependents, performance related bonus, etc. We also offer a range of employee health and wellbeing programs including our truly unique Partnership for Prevention program, a global commitment by GSK to provide all employees with up to 40 preventative healthcare services bringing to life our mission to: do more, feel better and live longer.
At GSK we value diversity and treat all candidates equally. We aim to create an inclusive workplace where all employees feel engaged, supportive of one another, and know their work makes an important contribution.
If you would like to join us in innovating and inspiring changes in the industry, we urge you to apply online.
*LI-GSK
Why GSK?Uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together.
GSK is a global biopharma company with a purpose to unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. Title: Senior Manager, Country HR
Description:
This is a job posting on behalf of our subsidiary, PT Hok Tong.
Halcyon Agri Corporation Limited is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Hainan Natural Rubber Industry Group, a publicly listed company in Singapore. Headquartered in Singapore, the group employs over 14,000 peoples across over 100 locations worldwide. Leveraging an extensive network of plantations, factories, warehouses, terminals, and sales offices, the company distributes a wide range of natural rubber products to our customers worldwide.
We are looking for a Country HR Manager to be based in Palembang, Indonesia. The incumbent will oversee the full spectrum of human resources functions for our companies in Indonesia.  He/she will be responsible for providing HR leadership, ensuring compliance with Indonesian employment regulations, and driving HR initiatives aligned with the Group’s overall business objectives.
 Key Responsibilities
1. HR Leadership & Strategy
·        Oversee the HR function across all entities in Indonesia, ensuring alignment with group HR policies and local regulations.
·        Act as a strategic partner to business leaders, providing guidance on organizational design, workforce planning, and succession management.
·        Lead cultural integration and change management initiatives within the country.
2. Talent Management
·        Oversee recruitment and selection processes to ensure timely hiring of qualified candidates.
·        Develop and oversee learning and development initiatives to strengthen organizational capabilities.
·        Manage performance management frameworks, ensuring clarity of KPIs and effective evaluation processes.
3. Employee Relations & Engagement
·        Oversee employee engagement programs to promote a positive, inclusive, and productive workplace.
·        Advise on employee relations issues, ensuring fair and consistent application of HR policies.
·        Manage grievance and disciplinary processes in accordance with Indonesian labor law.
·        Together with the Industrial Relations department, lead the team in resolving and/or conducting negotiations with the Labor Union or the Government in the event of labor disputes
4. Compensation, Benefits & Payroll
·        Oversee compensation and benefits programs, ensuring competitiveness and compliance with statutory requirements.
·        Manage payroll operations and statutory reporting for all Indonesian entities.
·        Provide market insights to Group HR on salary structures and incentive frameworks.
5. Compliance & HR Operations
·        Oversee HR administration, employee records, and contract management.
·        Ensure compliance with Indonesian employment laws, labor regulations, and government reporting requirements.
·        Coordinate with legal and external authorities on HR-related statutory matters.
6. Group HR Collaboration
·        Work closely with Group HR to implement global policies and systems in Indonesia.
·        Provide country-specific insights to support group-wide HR initiatives and strategic decision-making.
·        Ensure harmonization of HR practices across the group while accommodating local needs.
 Qualifications & Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field. Master’s degree or HR certification (e.g., SHRM, CIPD, GPHR) is an advantage.
Minimum 8–10 years of progressive HR experience, with at least 3–5 years in a managerial role in Indonesia.
Strong knowledge of Indonesian labor laws, HR best practices, and market trends.
Proven track record in managing HR functions within multinational or group company structures.
Excellent interpersonal, communication, and leadership skills with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels.
Language proficiency in Mandarin and English is required (both spoken and written for the correspondence with China headquarter), ability to communicate in Bahasa Indonesia would be an added advantage.
Ability to work independently, adapt to a fast-paced environment, and manage multiple priorities.

Title: Building & Facility Management Analyst - Sumatera
Description:
About Amartha
At Amartha, we empower micro-businesses across Indonesia, enabling growth and equal prosperity. We’ve supported over 2.7 million entrepreneurs—mostly women—by disbursing IDR 22.8 trillion in funding. As we step into 2025, Amartha is evolving into a technology-driven financial ecosystem, expanding our reach in lending, funding, and payments. About the Role
The Building Management role involves overseeing and coordinating the operations and maintenance of multiple buildings or facilities. This position is responsible for ensuring the efficient operation, proper maintenance, and compliance with safety and regulatory standards for all buildings . It requires a strategic approach to managing resources, budgets, and teams to optimize building performance while ensuring high levels of tenant satisfaction.

Preparing and carrying out emergency protocols and procedures.
Maintaining a safe environment for building occupants and visitors.
Preparing and monitoring maintenance budgets.
Negotiating and liaising with third-party service providers.

Ensuring facilities are in compliance with applicable policies, regulations, and building codes.
Responding to inquiries and requests by building tenants and resolving any problems or issues.
Requirements
Bachelor's degree in any field.
At least 3 years of experience as a building manager or in a similar role.
Knowledge of relevant laws, regulations, and building management.
Excellent project management and negotiation skills.
Good communication and customer service skills.
Strong critical thinking and problem-solving skills
At Amartha, we are dedicated to creating a workplace that celebrates diversity, ensures equity, and fosters inclusion. We believe that diverse perspectives—shaped by factors such as gender, age, race, ethnicity, education, culture, and life experiences—drive innovation and growth.
We actively welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us in building an environment where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered. Our commitment is to provide equal opportunities and foster a sense of belonging that enables our employees to thrive and make meaningful contributions.

Title: Regional Acquisition Team Leader
Description:
Amartha is a technology company focused on creating shared prosperity by developing digital financial solutions for the grassroots economy. Founded in 2010 as a microfinance institution, Amartha connects rural, women-led micro-enterprises with affordable capital. Amartha is expanding as a tech company, building a microfinance ecosystem that connects to the growing digital economy through capital, investment, and payment services. By boosting the competitiveness of micro and small entrepreneurs, Amartha empowers women-led MSMEs, creates jobs, and fosters inclusive economic growth.
About the role
As a Regional Acquisition Team Leader, you will be instrumental in leading and optimizing the performance of our field acquisition teams within your designated territories across Indonesia. This role focuses on strategically driving expansion of our Amarthalink agent network through effective field engagement, specifically targeting the integration of LKD Agents, local Warung/Toko Kelontong and Konter Pulsa into the Amartha ecosystem. You will be responsible for the comprehensive oversight of field operations, rigorous analysis of performance metrics, and the delivery of actionable strategic insights to maximize the efficiency and scalability of our field acquisition initiatives. In this role, you will have the opportunity to directly impact the growth and scalability of Amartha's agent network through strategic field operations. We are looking for a driven individual who thrives in complex, evolving environments and is passionate about driving growth through traditional acquisition channels. If you have a knack for strategy and a passion for field operations, we encourage you to apply.
Scope of Responsibilities:
Provide direct oversight and leadership to field acquisition teams within assigned territories.
Accountable for ensuring that new Monthly Transacting Users (MTU) acquisition and Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) targets are consistently met on a monthly basis.
Monitor and ensure that field team operational fulfillment aligns with established targets and standards.
Implement and enforce stringent measures to prevent and mitigate fraudulent activities within the field team.
Requirements
1-3 years of experience in field sales, sales operations, business development, or team management, preferably within the financial services, e-commerce, or FMCG sectors.
Demonstrated success in leading and motivating field-based teams to achieve and exceed targets.
Proven ability to implement and enforce compliance and risk management protocols in field operations.
Familiarity with the Indonesian market landscape, particularly the LKD Agents, Warung/Toko Kelontong and Konter Pulsa segments, is highly desirable.
Willingness to travel extensively within assigned territories
Preferred Skills:
Background in retail, telecommunications, or a related sector with a heavy reliance on field acquisition strategies.
Strong understanding of field sales methodologies, performance management, and operational efficiency.
Good analytical skills with the ability to interpret data, identify trends, and develop data-driven strategies.
Exceptional communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills, with the ability to build rapport and influence stakeholders.
Proficiency in CRM and sales management software to track field operations.
At Amartha, we are dedicated to creating a workplace that celebrates diversity, ensures equity, and fosters inclusion. We believe that diverse perspectives—shaped by factors such as gender, age, race, ethnicity, education, culture, and life experiences—drive innovation and growth.
We actively welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us in building an environment where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered. Our commitment is to provide equal opportunities and foster a sense of belonging that enables our employees to thrive and make meaningful contributions.

About the role

We are looking for an experienced Regional General Manager to oversee our Tecno Indonesia operations in Makassar, South Sulawesi. As the Regional General Manager, you will be responsible for managing and developing our sales and distribution channels in the region, with a focus on driving growth and profitability. This is a contract role with opportunities for extension or conversion to a permanent position.
What you'll be doing
Develop and implement regional sales and distribution strategies to achieve revenue and growth targets
Manage and lead a team of sales representatives and distribution partners to deliver exceptional customer service and sales performance
Monitor and analyse market trends, competitive activities and customer feedback to inform decision-making and drive continuous improvement
Identify and capitalise on new business opportunities to expand our presence and market share in the region
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure the successful launch and promotion of new products and services
Oversee budgeting, forecasting and reporting for the regional operations
Represent the company and build strong relationships with key stakeholders, including customers, partners and industry associations
What we're looking for
Significant experience (8+ years) in a sales management or regional leadership role within the consumer electronics or telecommunications industry
Proven track record of achieving sales and revenue targets through effective team leadership and channel management
Excellent understanding of the Makassar and South Sulawesi markets, including customer needs, competitive landscape and distribution channels
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to use data to drive decision-making
Effective communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build and maintain relationships at all levels
Proficiency in Bahasa Indonesia and English, both verbal and written
What we offer

At PT Transisi Mobile Indonesia, we are committed to providing our employees with a supportive and rewarding work environment. As the Regional General Manager, you will have the opportunity to play a key role in shaping the growth and success of our Tecno brand in the region. We offer competitive remuneration, opportunities for career development, and a range of benefits to support your work-life balance.
About us

PT Transisi Mobile Indonesia is a leading consumer electronics company with a strong presence in Indonesia. As the exclusive distributor of the Tecno brand, we are dedicated to providing high-quality, affordable mobile devices and accessories to our customers. With a focus on innovation and customer satisfaction, we are committed to driving growth and expansion in the Indonesian market.
Apply now to join our dynamic team and be a part of the exciting journey ahead!

Title: IT Network & Security
Company: ADR Group Of Companies (PT Selamat Sempurna Tbk.)
Description:
Job Description:
1. Design, configure, and maintain corporate network infrastructure, including routers, switches, firewalls, and other network devices.
2. Monitor and optimize network performance, troubleshoot, resolve and mitigate any risk related to network operation and its security. Take proactive measures to optimize network performance, security and minimize downtime.
3. Maintain up-to-date documentation for network diagrams, configurations, and incident response plan 4. Develop and implement security policies, procedures, and standards to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of our systems and data
5. Monitor and manage security, keep up network security measures to protect data, systems, and network infrastructure from unauthorized access and cyber threats. This includes managing firewall rules and setting up a VPN
6. Perform security assessments and audits to identify vulnerabilities, threats, and risks to our infrastructure and apply security patches
7. Conduct regular security awareness training and education sessions for staff members to promote a culture of cybersecurity awareness 8. Stay up to date with the latest security trends, threats, and technologies.
Qualification:
1. Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field
2. In-depth understanding of network topologies, protocols, and technologies, including TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, IPSec, Tunneling, VLAN, SD-WAN and routing switching
3. Familiar and able to configure network devices such us router, switch, access point, firewall (Cisco, Mikrotik, etc)
4. Minimal 3 years of experience in network security engineering, with a strong understanding of network protocols, firewalls, VPNs, and other security technologies
5. Experience deploying and managing endpoint security solutions (e.g. management frameworks, EDR tools)
6. Familiarity with security frameworks and compliance regulations (e.g., ISO/IEC 27001, NIST)
7. Excellent teamwork abilities and Strong communication skills to work effectively with crossfunctional teams
8. Ability to work flexible hours and be on-call for network emergencies

A degree in Animal Nutrition, Veterinary Science, or a closely related field
At least 2 years of experience working as a Pet Nutritionist/R&D Pet Care/Pet Care Formulation Development or in a similar role, preferably in a veterinary or pet care setting
Extensive knowledge of pet nutrition, including the specific dietary needs of various breeds and life stages
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively educate and advise pet owners
Strong problem-solving and critical-thinking abilities to identify and address complex nutritional challenges
Passion for animal welfare and a commitment to providing the highest standard of care for our furry clients

Title: Procurement Officer
Description:

As a Procurement Officer, you will play a crucial role in supporting our global supply chain operations and ensuring the timely procurement of goods and services to meet the needs of our business. This is a full-time position, offering the opportunity to work with a talented and collaborative team in our central Jakarta office.
What you'll be doing
Manage the end-to-end procurement process, including sourcing, negotiating, and contracting with vendors
Conduct market research to identify new suppliers and negotiate favourable pricing and terms
Monitor and maintain inventory levels, ensuring timely replenishment and optimal stock levels
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business requirements and source appropriate products and services
Implement and maintain efficient procurement procedures and policies
Analyse procurement data and provide recommendations to improve processes and reduce costs
Stay up-to-date with market trends, regulations, and best practices in the procurement field
What we're looking for
Minimum 2 - 3 years of experience in a procurement or supply chain role in any Industry, preferably experience handling procurement in Facility Services.
Strong negotiation and vendor management skills with the ability to build and maintain relationships with suppliers
Proficiency in data analysis and the use of procurement software and tools
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams
Ability to work under pressure, prioritise tasks, and meet deadlines
Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, or a related field
What we offer

At PT. OCS Global Services (OCS Indonesia), we are committed to providing a supportive and rewarding work environment for our employees. In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a range of benefits, including:
Comprehensive health insurance coverage
Opportunities for professional development and career growth
Flexible work arrangements to maintain a healthy work-life balance
Team-building activities and social events to foster a collaborative culture

About us

PT. OCS Global Services (OCS Indonesia) is a leading provider of integrated supply chain solutions in the Manufaktur, Transportasi & Logistik industry. With a strong presence across Indonesia, we are committed to delivering excellence to our customers through innovative technologies, sustainable practices, and a talented team of professionals. Join us in shaping the future of procurement and logistics in Indonesia.
Apply now to become our next Procurement Officer and be part of our dynamic team!

Title: Customer Service and Operation Specialist (Airfreight)

The ideal candidate will possess a strong background in freight forwarding and a passion for delivering exceptional customer service.  Customer Support

Shipment Coordination

Problem Solving

Documentation and Compliance
- Prepare and handle all hands-on documentation from end to end, to ensure compliance with international regulations and company policies.

Collaboration
- Work closely with the teams to provide a seamless customer experience.
- Assist in the development of operational improvements to enhance service delivery.
Performance Tracking
- Monitor customer feedback and service metrics, contributing to the continuous improvement of processes and procedures.
  Qualifications
- Degree in Logistics, or a related field preferred.
- Minimum of 3 years of experience in air freight forwarding export and import operation. Lastest working experience is freight forwarder.
- Strong master of air freight forwarding operations, documentation handling, regulations, and industry practices. 
- Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with a strong customer service orientation.
- Detail-oriented with strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
- Proficiency in freight forwarding software (specifically Cargowise) and Microsoft Office Suite.
 What We Offer
- Competitive salary and promotion package.
- Opportunities for rapid business development and career growth.
- A collaborative and supportive work environment.

Title: Continuous Improvement Engineer Staff
Description:
🌟 Shape Your Bright Career with Us! 🌟
Eka Jaya Group (Hanasui, NCo) is #Hiring for the Continuous Improvement Engineer Staff position!
About Us:
Eka Jaya Group is a leader in the beauty industry, proudly home to premium brands Hanasui and NCo. Since our establishment in December 2016, we've built a strong foundation based on our core values: Integrity, Compassion, Innovation, and Customer Satisfaction (IC²). With our private factory, we guarantee superior product quality, timely delivery, and competitive pricing.
We’re seeking passionate individuals to join our dynamic team as we continue to grow and strengthen our brands in the beauty industry.
Position: Continuous Improvement Engineer Staff
Work Location: Cikupa, Kabupaten Tangerang.
Job Requirements:s
Minimum of a Bachelor's Degree (S1) in Industrial Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or another related field
Have a minimum of 3-5 years of work experience in Continuous Improvement, Process Engineering, or a similar role within a manufacturing environment. 
In-depth understanding of Lean Manufacturing principles (Kaizen, 5S, VSM, Kanban, Poka-Yoke)
Strong data analysis skills using software such as Microsoft Excel (advanced) or any tools
Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills to influence and collaborate with people at all levels.
Leadership and facilitation skills to lead teams and workshops.
Change Management: Ability to manage resistance to change and drive the adoption of new processes.
Proactive, self-directed, and results-oriented.
Job Responsibilities:
Process Analysis & Opportunity Identification: Conduct direct observations on the production floor (Gemba walks) to understand workflows, identify inefficiencies, and discover potential improvements. Analyze production data (such as OEE, scrap rate, cycle time, downtime) to pinpoint problem areas and negative trends. Lead or facilitate Value Stream Mapping (VSM) sessions to map current state processes and design more efficient future state conditions.
Improvement Project Management: Lead improvement projects from inception to completion using frameworks like DMAIC (Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control) or PDCA (Plan, Do, Check, Act). Form and lead cross-functional teams (Production, Maintenance, Quality, Logistics) to execute improvement projects. Manage project timelines, resources, and budgets to ensure projects are completed on time and meet their targets.
Lean & Six Sigma Implementation: Serve as the primary expert and facilitator for Lean tools, including Kaizen, 5S, Standardized Work, and Poka-Yoke. Apply statistical tools and Six Sigma methodologies to analyze root causes and reduce process variability. Collaborate with engineering and production teams to ensure that new or modified process designs incorporate Lean principles.
CI Culture Training & Development: Develop and deliver training to employees at all levels on the fundamental concepts of Lean, Six Sigma, and other improvement tools. Coach and mentor teams or individuals in carrying out their improvement initiatives. Actively promote and cultivate a continuous improvement mindset throughout the factory.
Performance Measurement & Reporting: Establish Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for each improvement project and track its progress. Validate project outcomes and calculate the cost savings or efficiency gains achieved. Create and present project progress reports to management regularly.
Why Join Eka Jaya Group?
At Eka Jaya Group, we empower our team members to learn, grow, and make an impact. If you're looking for an exciting opportunity in a dynamic and fast-paced environment, #EJINIAN is the place for you! Join us and contribute to the growth of Hanasui and NCo—two beloved brands that are making waves in the beauty industry.
Upsize your career with us at #LIFEatEJI
Let’s #EJITOTHEMOON together! 🚀

Title: Import Planning Analyst
Description:
Import Planning Analyst to manage the end-to-end import process of finished good tires. Plays a crucial role in ensuring the smooth, efficient, and compliant flow of imported finished good tires.
Import Process Management:
▪Plan, coordinate, and execute all import activities from order placement with suppliers to final delivery at the distribution center or warehouse.
▪Prepare, review, and manage all necessary import documentation (e.g., commercial invoices, packing lists, bills of lading, certificates of origin, customs declarations) to ensure accuracy and compliance for Import tires.
▪Collaborate closely with sales, inventory management, and warehousing teams to understand import requirements, sales forecasts, and delivery schedules for Import tires.
Logistics & Vendor Management:
▪Coordinate with logistics providers (ocean carriers, air freight, trucking companies) to optimize transportation routes, modes, and costs for large volumes of Import tires, including container optimization and consolidation.
▪Track and trace inbound shipments, providing regular updates to relevant stakeholders on delivery status and potential delays for Import tire inventory.
Cost & Efficiency Optimization:
▪Identify opportunities for cost reduction in import logistics, duties, and fees for Import tires without compromising service levels or compliance.
▪Analyze import lead times and identify bottlenecks or areas for process improvement to enhance supply chain efficiency for Import tire distribution.
▪Propose and implement strategies to mitigate risks associated with international shipping and customs for Import tire supply chains.

Data Analysis & Reporting:
▪Maintain accurate records of all import transactions, costs, and performance metrics for Import tires.
▪Prepare regular reports on import volumes, costs, lead times, and compliance status for management review, specifically focusing on the impact on Import tire availability and sales.
▪Utilize data to identify trends, forecast future import needs, and support strategic decision-making for Import tire supply chain optimization.
Qualification:
Strong understanding of international trade regulations, customs, and Incoterms
Proficient in logistics systems and ERP (e.g., SAP)
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and negotiation skills
Detail-oriented with high accuracy and compliance mindset
Able to work independently and in cross-functional teams
Strong communication skills in English, both written and verbal
Requirement:
Minimum D3 from any major
Minimum grade A3 / T3 or above
Experienced in demand and supply planning such as Production Planning, Industrial Engineering and Process Improvement.
Proficient in Ms. Excel & Ms. Word
Fluent in Bahasa Indonesia & English (spoken and written)
Understands manufacturing business processes

Title: Assistant Manager, Warehouse & Logistics (Tobacco - Malang Placement)
Description:
The Assistant Manager, Warehouse & Logistics plays a critical role in overseeing day-to-day operations within the warehouse and logistic processes. This position involves the management of goods movement, from receiving, storing to shipping, and ensuring operations in compliance with internal standards and industry regulations. The role involves supervising warehouse and logistic teams, maintaining inventory accuracy, optimizing space utilization, inbound and outbound deliveries, and supporting continuous improvement in warehouse and logistics performance.
RESPONSIBILITIES:
Warehouse
Manage daily warehouse operations including receiving, inspection, storage, handling and dispatch of all raw materials, finished goods and spare parts
Oversee and ensure the accuracy of documentation for incoming and outgoing goods, including stock labelling and system updates.
Implement and improve warehouse strategies, policies, and procedures to support overall operational goals.
Ensure effective warehouse layout and optimize space usage while considering future capacity requirements.
Managing materials shelf life by tracking expiration dates and implementing procedures to minimize the risk of materials expiring or deteriorating
Support the implementation and maintenance of warehouse standards in line with company quality systems and industry compliance.
Maintain inventory accuracy through routine cycle counts, stock reconciliations, and system audits.
Investigating and resolving any inventory discrepancies found during cycle counts
Logistic
Overseeing the inbound and outbound deliveries and ensuring the goods reach the right place at the right time and cost-effectively
Monitor and analyse logistics costs and identify opportunities for process improvements and cost reduction.
Oversee and ensure the accuracy of documentation for domestic, export and import deliveries and comply with regulations.
Coordinate logistics support for internal departments and external parties as required.
General
Ensure compliance with Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) regulations and company policies.
Develop and lead implementation for cost down, optimizations and innovations activities
Lead and develop team member to ensure a productive and safety-focused work environment.
Willing to do ad hoc assignment or take up cross function support
REQUIREMENTS:
Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain, Logistics, Industrial Engineering, or a related field.
Minimum 3 years of experience in warehouse and logistic or supply chain management, preferably in the tobacco or FMCG industry.
In-depth knowledge of tobacco raw materials, storage conditions, and quality inspection standards will be an advantage
Hands-on experience in tobacco warehouse operations is highly preferred.
Proficient in warehouse management systems (WMS)
Knowledge in ERP system will be an advantage
Knowledge in export and import procedure and regulations
Knowledge in Custom & Excise system & regulation will be an advantage
Strong leadership, people management, and team development skills.
Excellent problem-solving and communication abilities.
High attention to detail, accuracy, and safety awareness.
Proactive, adaptable, and committed to continuous improvement.
Good communication skills in English (written and verbal)
Intermediate Microsoft Excel Skill

Title: Store Supervisor (Penempatan Jawa)
Description:
WE’RE HIRING!
STORE SUPERVISOR
Based in Java | Full-Time
ABOUT US:
Established in 2024 in Malang, Indonesia, PT Dua Pendekar Ayam is the powerhouse behind Dikichi — a bold new brand on a mission to serve high-quality, affordable fried chicken to the masses. Our ambition? To become Indonesia’s No. 1 fried chicken brand and take it to the world.

ABOUT THE ROLE:
As a Store Supervisor, you’ll be the engine behind smooth store operations. You’ll ensure everything — from sales to sanitation — runs at the highest standard. You’ll supervise the crew, manage inventory, support promotions, and take full ownership of your store’s day-to-day performance to create a consistent, high-quality customer experience.
This is a field-based, full-time role to be based across Java. You’ll work closely with our Store Manager and our Crew Operations Team — playing a critical role in delivering the Dikichi experience that wins customers' hearts (and cravings).
WHAT YOU’LL DO:
Ensure Smooth Store Operations – From opening to closing, make sure the store runs efficiently and delivers the Dikichi experience.

Lead & Supervise the Crew – Supervise day-to-day crew activities, monitor performance, and give clear, constructive feedback.
Maintain Store Cleanliness – Ensure hygiene and cleanliness of the store and surrounding areas at all times.
Own Inventory & Stock – Manage daily stock levels, monitor usage, and prevent loss or overstock.
Understand P&L & Operational Metrics – Monitor store profitability, optimize costs, and report on store KPIs.
YOU’RE A GREAT FIT IF YOU:
Hold a Diploma (D3) or Bachelor Degree (S1) from any major
Have 1+ year experience in F&B operational management, preferably in a well-known fast-food brand
Experienced in leading teams of 60+ crew members in a very high-volume store
Are skilled in managing store inventory and also daily operations
Thrive in team-based environments and are ready to work with a strong work ethic
Are communicative, energetic, reliable, proactive, and possess strong leadership qualities
Are willing to work in shifts
Are ready to join immediately and open to placement across all regions in Java
This isn’t your typical supervisor role. It’s a stepping stone to real leadership at the frontlines. We’re looking for bold talents ready to grow fast, lead teams, and make a real impact on the ground.

Ability to communicate complex technical data in both English and the local language.
Creative, dynamic, and resilient, with a strong focus on project management and data analysis.
Capable of managing knowledge and building effective relationships with both internal and external partners.

Perform troubleshooting of test instruments and coordinate repairs or servicing with vendors when necessary.
Contribute to continuous improvement projects in the areas of calibration process efficiency, lab test method optimization, or digital system integration.
Perform laboratory testing for incoming materials, in-process samples, finished products, and product development validation.
Conduct laboratory tests to support product verification, quality inspection, failure analysis, and engineering evaluations.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (QA, production, R&D) in investigations, validations, and product testing.
Develop, implement, and maintain test methods and standard operating procedures (SOPs) for laboratory operations.
Conduct environmental, mechanical, and functional testing (e.g., reliability, aging, stability, or performance testing).
Operate and maintain laboratory equipment and instruments, ensuring proper functionality and calibration status.
Document test results in compliance with Good Documentation Practices (GDP) and applicable regulatory requirements.
Support method validation, equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ), and sample management processes.
Contribute to laboratory safety programs and ensure compliance with EHS (Environment, Health, and Safety) guidelines.

You're the right fit if:
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Biomedical, or related field).
Familiarity with ISO 17025, ISO 13485, and FDA QSR requirements.
Experience using calibration software or systems (e.g., CMMS, GAGEtrak, Indysoft  or similar).
Knowledge in metrology, measurement uncertainty, and instrumentation (pressure, temperature, electrical, etc.).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Good communication skills in English (written and verbal).

How We Work Together
We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.

Onsite roles require full-time presence in the company’s facilities.

Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.

About Philips.
We are a health technology company. We built our entire company around the belief that every human matters, and we won't stop until everybody everywhere has access to the quality healthcare that we all deserve. Do the work of your life to help the lives of others.

Title: Documentation Specialist

Identifying areas for improvement in the documentation process and implementing solutions.

Attention to detail: Essential for ensuring accuracy in documentation. 
Organisational skills: Necessary for managing and tracking numerous documents and shipments.

Knowledge of international trade regulations: Understanding of customs procedures, shipping documentation, and relevant laws and regulations. 
Proficiency in relevant software: Familiarity with logistics management systems, word processing, and spreadsheet software

A Team Player who can work and collaborate with the local and HQ teams.

Title: Lab Analyst
Description:
Job Description Summary :
The Lab Analyst will be responsible for conducting laboratory tests and experiments to analyze various materials, substances, and products. The role involves following established protocols, ensuring accuracy and compliance with safety standards, and assisting with the maintenance of lab equipment and supplies. This position requires strong attention to detail, critical thinking, and a methodical approach to work.

Job Description :
Testing and Analysis:
Perform routine and non-routine laboratory tests to analyze samples according to established protocols and specifications.
Record test results, analyze data, and report findings in a clear and concise manner.
Equipment Management:
Operate and maintain laboratory equipment and instruments, ensuring they are calibrated and in good working condition.
Troubleshoot and resolve equipment issues as needed.
Quality Control:
Ensure all analyses comply with relevant regulatory standards, guidelines, and quality control procedures.
Perform and document calibration and validation of test instruments and equipment.
Safety and Compliance:
Adhere to safety protocols and regulatory requirements, maintaining a clean and safe working environment.
Maintain proper disposal and storage of chemicals and waste products.
Documentation and Reporting:
Maintain accurate, detailed laboratory notebooks and electronic records.
Prepare reports summarizing results and observations for internal teams or external clients.
Collaboration and Support:
Work with other lab team members and departments to complete projects and resolve technical issues.
Assist with the development of new testing methods or improvements to existing procedures.

Job Requirement :
Min Bachelor’s degree in Chemistry
Min 1 year of experience in a Lab Analyst
Strong knowledge of laboratory techniques, equipment, and safety protocols.
Proficient in using laboratory instruments and software (e.g., HPLC, spectrophotometers, microscopes).
Ability to analyze data and write reports effectively.
Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
Certification in Laboratory Safety (e.g., OSHA, GLP) is a plus.
Willing to be located in Riau (Lubuk Gaung)

Title: Agronomy Research Assistant
Description:
Job Description Summary :
Join our team as an Agronomy research assistant specializing in Mineral Nutrition Management for oil palm. You’ll design and conduct projects on fertilizer efficiency and also execute various methods on planting material screening based on nutrient efficiency. If you’re a skilled researcher passionate about sustainable agriculture while improving yield, apply now.
 Job Description :
Work on a number of research projects in the scope of mineral nutrition of oil palm, including but not limited to fertilizer efficiency trials, planting material characterization, plant nutrient allocation and efficiency of different planting material trials. 
Execute various methods or experiments for characterizing oil palm planting materials based on nutrient efficiency.
Collect, analyse, interpret data and build technical reports the operational team or the top management.
Develop research reports and make presentations on findings, either non-scientific or scientific reports.
Work collaboratively with other members of research team, either within department, inter departments or with other parties, internally or externally.

Job Requirements :
Minimum Master (S-2) in Agriculture (soil) with relevant bachelor degree (S-1).
Willing to be placed in our main research centre located in Riau-Sumatra
Experience working in a commercial or industry environment performing field trials is preferable, but a fresh graduate with a strong related research background is also welcome.
Experience working/study in plant metabolomics will be also considered a plus.
Have a high interest in research on plant nutrition and also characterization based on nutrient efficiency, especially in oil palm.
Proficiency with statistical analysis and data visualization (R, SPSS, Minitab, etc.).
Good communication skill either oral or written English is highly preferred.
Good logical and critical thinking, proactive, flexible, quick to adapt and willing to meet deadlines.
Ability to work independently and as a part of a team, to manage and prioritize projects simultaneously.

Description:
About the company
FERMEX Group as a First Mover in Indonesia for Yeast & Bakery Ingredients Product, has established since 1976. Our company is affiliate legal entity between AB MAURI UK & CITRA BONANG GROUP.
PT JAYA FERMEX as part of FERMEX Group which focused on distributes our best and excellent bakery ingredients products and serves our customers around Indonesia. Currently, we have 17 branches and 23 agent/ distributors, and operates in major cities in Indonesia.
We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Branch Manager to lead our branch operations and achieve sales targets. You will play a key role in driving branch revenue, transactions, and customer growth while ensuring full compliance with Know Your Customer (KYC) regulations.

Assist sales team in negotiating with customers

Handle and resolve customer complaints
Periodically review the sales territory mapping based on sub-districts/ visits to be more effective in the field

REQUIREMENTS :
Bachelor's degree or master's degree in any major 
Minimum 3 years experiences in bakery ingredients, food ingredients, FMCG company
Strong knowledge in sales, finance and supply chain
Good in computer literacy, microsoft office and other software  
Strong leadership skills to guide teams and achieve business goals
Effective communication skills with customers, other departments, and team members
The ability to address customer concerns, resolve issues, and build loyalty
Candidate is willing to travel for business propose
