Title: Brach Manager
Description:
About the role

We are seeking an exceptional Branch Manager to join our team at PT Mitra Megah Profitamas' in South Kalimantan. This full-time position is a crucial role that will be responsible for supporting the overall operations of the business and contributing to its strategic growth.
What you'll be doing
Develop strategic plans, policies, direction, and branch targets in accordance with the instructions and directives provided by Management regarding the 3S (Sales, Service, Spareparts).
Responsible for the branch's achievements and performance in sales operations, administration and service, and human resource development by planning, monitoring, and evaluating in accordance with Management's targets.
Establish sales targets and be responsible for achieving sales targets, market share, and branch profits based on the results of the analysis.
Improve branch productivity and performance, as well as customer satisfaction, and maintain good customer relationships.
Coordinate, supervise, and be responsible for coaching, development, and branch activities.
Motivate and maintain intensive communication with Sales Heads and Service Managers.
Provide effective reporting on a regular and periodic basis.
What we're looking for
Minimum 5 years of experience in Sales and Marketing Manager or related position
Experience at Automotive Dealership R4 is a big plus
Strong understanding of operational best practices and the ability to drive process improvements
Excellent problem-solving, decision-making, and critical thinking skills
Proven track record of effective team leadership and people management
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with stakeholders at all levels
Proficient in using relevant business software and technology tools
Degree in Business Administration, Operations Management or a related field
What we offer

At PT Mitra Megah Profitamas', we are dedicated to providing our employees with a supportive and rewarding work environment. In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a comprehensive benefits package, including health insurance, retirement contributions, and opportunities for career advancement. We also prioritise work-life balance, with flexible scheduling and opportunities to work remotely when appropriate. If you are passionate about leading a dynamic retail team and driving business growth, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity.

Title: Taskforce - Kalimantan
Description:
About Amartha
At Amartha, we empower micro-businesses across Indonesia, enabling growth and equal prosperity. We’ve supported over 2.7 million entrepreneurs—mostly women—by disbursing IDR 22.8 trillion in funding. As we step into 2025, Amartha is evolving into a technology-driven financial ecosystem, expanding our reach in lending, funding, and payments. Through innovation and digital solutions, we aim to enhance accessibility, streamline processes, and create a seamless user experience.
About the Role
The Taskforce role carries significant responsibility and autonomy, with the opportunity to drive improvements through initiatives and proposals. It plays a central role in both operations and the recovery process.
Back-Up Operations Responsibilities
Conduct surveys, validate, and disburse funds to partners at the point of contact
Perform daily closing activities at the point of contact
Handle administrative activities at the assigned point as the Branch Manager (BM)
Conduct visits and monitor the point team
Ensure that disbursement targets and Outstanding PAR (Problematic Account Rate) are achieved
In carrying out these duties and responsibilities, the taskforce is authorized to take initiatives and may propose:
Mapping of Majelis and Business Partners (BP)
Fulfillment of Business Partner needs
Other initiatives that support target achievement
Recovery Process Responsibilities
Manage the portfolio by identifying issues and implementing action strategies for resolution
Analyze issues at the point of contact
Provide feedback to LPR (Loan Processing Reconciliation) and HMB (Head of Management Branch) to take corrective actions
Provide guidance to ensure recovery targets are achieved (Flow rate and PAR improvement)
In carrying out these duties and responsibilities, the taskforce is authorized to take initiatives and may propose:
Mapping of Majelis and Business Partners (BP)
Proposals for BP fulfillment
Other strategic initiatives to support recovery and performance goals
Requirements
Willing to be placed at any Amartha branch across Indonesia for 3-6 months
At least 2 years of experience in the Grameen field or in another financial company
Experienced in collections and handling problematic customers
Knowledgeable in and able to implement business processes based on the Grameen model
Willing to travel and be stationed anywhere in Indonesia
At Amartha, we are dedicated to creating a workplace that celebrates diversity, ensures equity, and fosters inclusion. We believe that diverse perspectives—shaped by factors such as gender, age, race, ethnicity, education, culture, and life experiences—drive innovation and growth.
We actively welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us in building an environment where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered. Our commitment is to provide equal opportunities and foster a sense of belonging that enables our employees to thrive and make meaningful contributions.

Description:
Meratus Group is a leading integrated maritime and logistics operator in Indonesia, pioneering innovative solutions that drive efficiency and sustainability in the industry. With a rich history dating back to 1957, Meratus has evolved into a powerhouse, operating over 45 shipping routes, a fleet of 100 vessels, and a network of container terminals and logistics centers.
At Meratus, we are committed to digitalization, innovation, and transformation, ensuring seamless logistics and maritime services across Indonesia and Southeast Asia. Our customer-centric approach and agile operations empower businesses to navigate complex supply chain challenges with confidence.
We take pride in fostering a dynamic and inclusive workplace, where talented professionals can thrive and contribute to shaping the future of maritime logistics. Join us and be part of a team that is redefining industry standards while making a meaningful impact on global trade and sustainability.
Position Overview
Responsible for executing sales activities and preparing reports on sales visits as well as sales target achievements. Conduct customer satisfaction surveys and handle accounts receivable billing. Develop business growth by maintaining and expanding relationships with existing customers.
Responsibilities
Conduct sales activities to consistently achieve individual targets on a weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly basis.
Acquire new business from both corporate cargo owners and individual customers each month.
Perform regular sales visits to customers to gather information on their business conditions and monitor developments among their competitors.
Conduct customer satisfaction surveys during sales visits to ensure service quality.
Prepare and submit sales activity reports using the designated tools.
Manage billing for accounts receivable past the payment due date.
Maintain customer loyalty and ensure active transactions with existing clients.
Requirements
Bachelors degree in any field.
Preferably with at least 2 years of sales experience in forwarding or logistics, possessing advanced selling skills.
Preferably proficient in spoken and written English.
Possess a pleasant personality and strong communication skills.
Highly achievement-oriented, willing to travel, and able to work beyond regular office hours.
Committed to identifying and developing business opportunities through excellent client engagement and distinctive sales strategies.
Strong work ethic with the ability to work independently as well as collaboratively within a team.

Title: Operation Support Staff
Description:
Location: Banjarmasin, South Kalimantan
About the Role
As an Operational Support Staff, you will be instrumental in ensuring smooth agency operations by managing and monitoring critical documentation, executing administrative tasks accurately, and assisting with financial accountability. You will support field agents and operational workflows, ensuring timely updates and proper record-keeping.
What You’ll Be Doing
Create and maintain the daily job order update report, ensuring all operational activities are logged accurately and on time.
Prepare a job order receipts checklist to track missing supporting documents; follow up diligently on delayed items until all are received, then finalize the checklist.
Assist in creating the Cash Advance Disbursement Report, based on documentation and explanations from field agents.
Prepare supporting documents required for agency operations—such as Bills of Lading (BL), Manifests, Time Sheets, etc.—according to activity needs.
Support additional administration and documentation tasks to facilitate operational efficiency.
What We’re Looking For
Minimum educational background: Diploma (D-I/D-II/D-III) or bachelor’s degree (S1) in Administration, Logistics, Maritime, or a related field is preferred.
Previous experience in operational administration, ideally within shipping, logistics, or agency operations.
High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to meet deadlines.
Proficient in Microsoft Office (especially Excel and Word).
Good communication and coordination skills.
Willingness to be based in Banjarmasin, with flexibility to adapt to operational needs.
Great to Have
Experience with shipping or maritime documentation relevant to agency work.
Familiarity with maritime logistics terminology and workflows.
Able to work independently as well as within a team.

Title: Area Sales Manager (Multiple Locations : KALSELTENG or EAST JAVA)
Description:
About the role

We are seeking an experienced and driven Area Sales Manager to join our team at PT Penguin Indonesia. In this full-time role, you will be responsible for managing and growing sales across multiple direct & indirect channel, located in South & Central Kalimantan (KALSELTENG) area or East Java (JAWA TIMUR) area. As an integral part of our Building Material Products business, you will play a key role in achieving our ambitious sales targets and driving the success of our operations in the region.
What you'll be doing
Oversee the day-to-day operations and performance of direct & indirect channel, including multiple retail stores within your assigned area
Develop and implement effective sales strategies to maximize revenue and market share
Recruit, train, and manage high-performing sales teams to deliver exceptional customer service
Monitor and analyze sales data to identify trends, opportunities, and areas for improvement
Collaborate with the central marketing team to execute promotional campaigns and drive customer engagement
Ensure compliance with company policies, procedures, and industry regulations
Provide regular performance reports and recommendations to senior management
What we're looking for
Proven track record of success as an Area Sales Manager or similar role in the retail or consumer products industry
Excellent leadership and people management skills, with the ability to motivate and develop high-performing teams
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to use data to drive decision-making
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships with both internal and external stakeholders
Proficient in using sales and customer relationship management (CRM) software
Degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field

If you are excited about this opportunity and believe you have the skills and experience to succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply now.

Title: Teacher/Trainer
Description:
Position                          : Teacher/Trainer
Location                          : South Kalimantan
Employment Type        : Full-Time
  Job Description
Briton English Education, in collaboration with the Local Government of South Kalimantan, is seeking passionate and qualified professionals to join the Sekolah Berbahasa Inggris (SBI) program as English Trainers. The SBI program is an internationally aligned initiative designed to improve the English language proficiency and teaching competencies of both students and teachers, ensuring they meet minimum international standards. As an English Trainer, you will play a vital role in raising the quality of English education in South Kalimantan by delivering dynamic, communicative, and learner-centered instruction, while also supporting the professional development of local teachers.
 Key Responsibilities
Administer placement tests, monitor progress, conduct evaluations, and provide constructive feedback.
Deliver engaging and interactive English lessons aligned with CEFR and Cambridge qualifications.
Develop teachers’ competencies to meet Cambridge B1 and TKT standards.
Actively participate in regional academic events.
 Requirements
Bachelor's degree in English Education, English Literature, or related field
Minimum CEFR B2 level English proficiency (supported by IELTS, TOEFL, Cambridge, etc.)
Minimum 5 years of experience in English teaching or training
Preferably certified with TKT, CELTA, or other internationally recognized teaching credentials
Strong communication, interpersonal, and facilitation skills
Willingness to be based in or travel across South Kalimantan
Commitment to education and professionalism
 Benefits
• Competitive salary and work-related travel allowance (including flight tickets)
• Free accommodation during the assignment period
• Health insurance (BPJS)
• Regular training and professional development opportunities
• A chance to work in a nationally supported and internationally aligned education program
• Affiliation with Briton, one of Indonesia’s leading English institutions

Title: Account Executive (Pontianak)
Description:
About the Role
As an Account Executives, you will report to the Area Sales Manager, operating within a designated Area under the Region Operations team. This role requires close collaboration with the Region Sales team, including Merchant Strategy and Sales Support. She/he will regularly visit and collaborate with assigned merchants to assess their needs and provide tailored advice to increase revenue. Additionally, they will also collaborate across streams, including Operations and User Growth, to optimize results.
By joining us, you will be part of a mission-driven team that celebrates individual growth, fosters collaboration, and is passionate about making a meaningful difference for SMBs.
What You Will Do
Reached sales goals with assigned merchant leads by using Motion Standard and Virtual Engagement.
Proactively present solutions and conduct regular business reviews to help merchants expand their business through consultative selling.
Support merchants by identifying and resolving issues, including those related to merchant-funded promotions and advertising, troubleshooting, and modifying documentation or adding new outlets, entities, or brands.
Developing Joint Business Plan as collaborative action to grow merchants in the respected portfolio as the future growth driver.
Actively observe competitor movements and market movements.
Canvass regularly and onboard new merchants to increase market penetration.
Support the city's key initiatives, including Merchant Partnership, Assortment, and Merchant Spending.
Ensure timely submission and administration of advertisements, promotions, agreement changes, and additional outlets, including merchant complaints. Always report to the Area Sales Manager and seek assistance from the appropriate Person In Charge.
Develop skills, learn about new products, and attend training regularly.
What You Will Need
Holds a Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Sales, or a related field.
At least 2 years of relevant experience, preferably in a top 5 FMCG, telecommunications, or tech company, with over 1 year spent in a representative area and possessing a strong network and knowledge of the region.
Demonstrates strong business sense with a solid understanding of markets, customers, and competitors.
Has existing relationships or the ability to bridge, develop, and maintain good relationships with key partners.
Possesses excellent communication and negotiation skills.
A fact-based, number-oriented individual who can interpret data effectively to make data-driven decisions.
Comfortable working under pressure in fast-paced and ambiguous environments, with a self-motivated and results-driven approach.
Willing to be placed in any location within Gojek’s area of operations.
About the Team
Account Executives drive sustainable revenue growth by developing merchant business. They build relationships, provide consultative solutions, onboard new merchants, and address their needs. They identify business opportunities, monitor market trends, support local sales, and ensure smooth administrative processes, while continuously developing their skills.
About GoTo Group
GoTo Group is the largest digital ecosystem in Indonesia with its mission to “Empower Progress’ by offering technological infrastructure and solutions for everyone to access and thrive in the digital economy. The GoTo ecosystem consists of on-demand transportation services, food and grocery delivery, logistics and fulfillment, as well as financial and payment services through the Gojek and GoTo Financial platforms.It is the first platform in Southeast Asia that hosts these crucial cases in a single ecosystem, capturing the majority of Indonesia’s vast consumer household.
About Gojek 
Gojek is Southeast Asia’s leading on-demand platform and pioneer of the multi-service ecosystem with over 2.5 million driver partners across the regions offering a wide range of services such as transportation, food delivery, logistics and more. With its mission to create impact at scale, Gojek is committed to resolving consumer problems and raising standards of living by connecting consumers to the best providers of goods and services in the market.
About GoTo Financial
GoTo Financial accelerates financial inclusion through its leading financial services and merchants solutions. Its consumer services include GoPay and GoPayLater and serve businesses of all sizes through Midtrans, Moka, GoBiz Plus, GoBiz, and Selly. With its trusted and inclusive ecosystem of products, GoTo Financial is open to new growth opportunities and aims to empower everyone to Make It Happen, Make It Together, Make It Last.
GoTo and its business units, including Gojek and GoToFinancial ("GoTo") only post job opportunities on our official channels on our respective company websites and on LinkedIn. GoTo is not liable for any job postings or job offers that did not originate from us. You should conduct your own due diligence to prevent being victims of any fake job scams, if they did not originate from GoTo's official recruitment channels.

Give insight on how the result of ongoing campaign and to suggest / give feedback on how to make it better & proposing campaign that can do better than existing / additional
Make sure all AM's Portfolio participate on campaign to achieve the topline target
Understand all ongoing campaign and do random checking to ensure AM understanding
Be a business consultant for AM & hard dealing merchant supposedly if AM can't handle the merchant & think of better sollution that can be given at the moment
Qualifications
What Essential Skills You Will Need
2+ Years of Experience in leading sales team, commercial and prior experience with leading an end-to-end sales cycle
Proficient with analytics and fluent with tools which help you achieve a clear understanding of data sets
Experience on managing a team
Experience establishing communication and engagement with prospects your ideas and suggestions in a clear and succinct way
Fluent in both Bahasa & English
Additional Information
Life at Grab
We care about your well-being at Grab, here are some of the global benefits we offer:
We have your back with Term Life Insurance and comprehensive Medical Insurance.
With GrabFlex, create a benefits package that suits your needs and aspirations.
Celebrate moments that matter in life with loved ones through Parental and Birthday leave, and give back to your communities through Love-all-Serve-all (LASA) volunteering leave
We have a confidential Grabber Assistance Programme to guide and uplift you and your loved ones through life's challenges.
Balancing personal commitments and life's demands are made easier with our FlexWork arrangements such as differentiated hours
What We Stand For At Grab
We are committed to building an inclusive and equitable workplace that provides equal opportunity for Grabbers to grow and perform at their best. We consider all candidates fairly and equally regardless of nationality, ethnicity, race, religion, age, gender, family commitments, physical and mental impairments or disabilities, and other attributes that make them unique.

Description:
Welcome to Our FUTURE LEADERS Programme for Store Supervisor !! If you are eager to learn new things and willing to take challenges, we will train you every skill needed in Operational Store and we will develop you as Store Manager.

What we offer
Our Store Management Trainee and Retail Management Trainee Programmes are designed to nurture and develop retail management talents, preparing individuals for long-term careers in store operations. If you have a passion for retail operations, this could be the perfect opportunity for you to gain comprehensive on-the-job training as well as intensive coaching and classroom teaching designed to equip you with vital store management skills.

The programme, lasting from 3 to 6 months depending on management skills, specific training needs of individuals and differing retail formats, offers hands-on store management exposure, covering customer service, cashiering, product knowledge, roster planning, budgeting and people management. Regular feedback sessions are also organized and mentors assigned to guide each trainee throughout the scheme.

REQUIREMENTS:
Fresh graduates are welcome to apply and must possess at least Bachelor's Degree in any field
Highly motivated, good interpersonal skill and eager to learn
Flexibility to work in store within store operating hours (under shift schedule, 6 days a week, including weekends and Public Holiday)

Title: Service Supervisor (Project Site)
Description:
Job Description:
Team Supervision & Leadership:
Supervise and lead a team of maintenance technicians and service staff, ensuring all tasks are performed in accordance with project timelines and safety standards.
Provide guidance and support in troubleshooting and diagnosing complex mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic issues on mining equipment.
Conduct performance reviews, provide training, and ensure adherence to operational procedures.
Maintenance Operations:
Oversee the routine maintenance and repair of all mining machinery, vehicles, and equipment to ensure operational efficiency.
Plan and execute preventative maintenance schedules and ensure all equipment is inspected regularly.
Respond promptly to unplanned breakdowns and manage emergency repairs to minimize operational downtime.
Coordinate with the parts department to ensure the availability of spare parts and tools for ongoing maintenance activities.
Safety & Compliance:
Ensure all maintenance work is performed in compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations, promoting a culture of safety across the team.
Conduct regular safety audits and inspections of equipment and maintenance areas, addressing any risks or non-compliance issues immediately.
Monitor adherence to the company’s safety protocols and industry standards.
Project Planning & Coordination:
Assist in the development of maintenance budgets, ensuring all activities are cost-effective and within financial constraints.
Manage the coordination of service schedules to minimize impact on project production and operational activities.
Collaborate with the project management team to develop strategies for minimizing equipment downtime and optimizing machinery life.
Reporting & Documentation:
Maintain detailed records of maintenance activities, service reports, and parts inventories.
Prepare regular maintenance and service reports for senior management, identifying issues, recommendations, and action plans.
Document and update procedures as necessary to reflect the latest maintenance practices and safety protocols.
Job Requirements:
Minimum Diploma or Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field.
Minimum Experience of 5+ years of hands-on experience in mining, construction, or heavy equipment maintenance.
At least 3 years in a supervisory or leadership role within a mining or industrial setting.

Title: Planner (Project Site)
Description:
Job Description:
Create detailed maintenance plans based on equipment manuals, historical data, and industry best practices
Coordinate and schedule preventive and corrective maintenance work to ensure equipment reliability and minimize production disruptions
Generate, review, and prioritize work orders, ensuring all necessary resources, tools, and spare parts are available
Manage spare parts inventory to ensure timely availability for planned maintenance activities
Track maintenance metrics (e.g., downtime, work completion rates) and suggest improvements for efficiency
Ensure that all maintenance work is carried out according to safety standards and regulatory requirements
Work closely with production, engineering, and other departments to align maintenance schedules with operational goals
Identify areas for improvement in maintenance processes and recommend solutions to enhance productivity and reliability
Maintain accurate records of all maintenance activities, including work orders, inspections, and equipment performance
Job Requirement:
Minimum diploma's degree from mechanical engineering or any major that related with the job
Minimum 3+ years of experience as planner in the mining industry
Willing to be placed in the project site

Title: Assistant Manager QC (Garment)
Description:
Requirement :

1.  Diploma / Bachelor’s degree (any major, preferably Garment Production or Fashion Design)
2. 8-10 years of experience in garment/apparel industry
3. Having experience in handling quality control as Asst. Manager, agile, communicative, and exhibit leadership qualities

Job Description:
- Support QC Manager in planning, implementing, and overseeing the factory’s quality control processes.
- Coordinate daily QC activities across cutting, sewing, finishing, and packing departments.
- Supervise QC team members, ensuring adherence to inspection standards and proper reporting.
- Implement quality standards based on buyer requirements, technical packs, and approved samples.
- Monitor in-line inspections to detect defects early and prevent bulk quality issues.
- Oversee end-line inspections to ensure finished garments meet required specifications.
- Ensure compliance with AQL (Acceptable Quality Level) and other customer-specific audit standards.
- Conduct pre-production meetings with production, IE, and QA teams to highlight key risk points.
- Assist in root cause analysis for recurring defects and initiate corrective/preventive actions (CAPA).
- Facilitate communication between QC inspectors and production supervisors to resolve quality issues quickly.
- Ensure accurate documentation of defects, inspection reports, and quality data for audits.
- Coordinate final inspection readiness and liaise with buyer/third-party auditors.
- Train QC staff on inspection techniques, SOPs, and buyer quality requirements.
- Work closely with the technical team to address construction, measurement, and finishing concerns.
- Ensure proper handling of rejections and oversee rework processes.
- Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance product quality and reduce defect rates.
- Maintain quality-related KPIs and report them to the QC Manager and senior management.
- Ensure compliance with safety, environmental, and ethical manufacturing practices during QC operations.
- JSS evaluation and coordination with related team.

Title: Education Counsellor
Description:
About IDP 
IDP is a global leader in international education services that helps people study abroad.
As a key voice in the industry, IDP partners with educational institutions to help them attract the right students and co-owns IELTS, a leading English proficiency test.
Established in 1969 in Australia, IDP has teams based in more than 60 countries. Despite its evolution, IDP’s purpose has remained constant: to help people transform their lives through international education. For more information, visit www.idp.com.
About Student Placement 
At IDP, it’s our goal to help every student fulfil their global potential by streamlining the pathway to studying abroad.
To do this, it takes scale. Each year, almost 100,000 IDP students are studying at more than 800 universities around the world, supported by our local teams who know each country’s education landscape inside and out.
Our unbiased guidance helps students build personalised pathways to success. IDP’s 2,200 counsellors reinforce their own expertise with data-driven insights to give advice that’s reliable and valuable.
For universities, governments and sector bodies, our quality insights and tailored solutions make it easier to reach ambitious international students that will shape the future of campuses worldwide.
About IELTS 
IDP is a proud co-owner of IELTS, the world’s best and most trusted English language test for study, work and migration. For more than 35 years, IELTS has been a pioneering force in the industry, and we continue to lead the sector today.
We believe in using technology to enhance human connection, rather than replace it. We want to empower our test takers to achieve their best score. We offer a choice of paper, computer or online testing, and the opportunity to retake just one skill if a test taker did not achieve their desired score, setting us apart from other high-stakes tests.
With an IELTS score, test takers can showcase their language proficiency and abilities and access more than 12,500 organisations worldwide – from education institutions and employers to government and professional bodies.
IDP ensures widespread accessibility by providing IELTS in more than 2,000 test locations in more than 60 countries.
Role purpose
The Education Counsellor will provide excellent counselling services to guide international students to find the institution that best meets their learning and career aspirations, including providing students with the right information and advice on Australia studies opportunities and local education system, culture and geographic region, assisting with application documentation, facilitating enrolments, and promoting IDP products and services.
Key accountabilities
Drive achievement of your pipeline goals, customer satisfaction, and advocacy targets through each stage of the student journey, including promoting student essential products and services, managing and maintaining leads and successful applications, seeking referral opportunities, encouraging and participating in event and lead generating activity, database maintenance.
Provide outstanding customer service to students, their families and education providers throughout the process, including participating in planning activities to build student engagement, providing students with the right information about admissions, processes and qualification options in a way they can understand easily, and proactively engaging with students and education providers regularly.
Required experience
Experience in a sales or customer service driven environment, including achieving individual and team based targets.
Good understanding of the education sector and placement of students into VET and universities, including immigration requirements and processes.
A Bachelor's Degree, Master's Degree / Post Graduate Degree, any field, Australia Graduate and/or prior experience working as an Education Counsellor or similar role is preferred.
Outstanding stakeholder management and communication skills, including fluency in English, both verbal and written.
Willing to work from our Yogyakarta Office.

BD is one of the largest global medical technology companies in the world. Advancing the world of health™ is our Purpose, and it’s no small feat. It takes the imagination and passion of all of us—from design and engineering to the manufacturing and marketing of our billions of MedTech products per year—to look at the impossible and find transformative solutions that turn dreams into possibilities. 

Why Join Us?

A career at BD means learning and working alongside inspirational leaders and colleagues who are equally passionate and committed to fostering an inclusive, growth-centered, and rewarding culture. You will have the opportunity to help shape the trajectory of BD while leaving a legacy at the same time.
To find purpose in the possibilities, we need people who can see the bigger picture, who understand the human story that underpins everything we do. We welcome people with the imagination and drive to help us reinvent the future of health. At BD, you’ll discover a culture in which you can learn, grow and thrive. And find satisfaction in doing your part to make the world a better place. 

Become a maker of possible with us!

Main Responsibilities
Maintain Strategic Relationships with Our Key Customer Accounts 
Proactively engage with existing customers to maintain strategic relationships  
Anticipate potential service problems and actively work towards addressing them 
Consolidate related data on customer satisfaction to drive better solutioning and recommendations  
Actively seek to understand the assigned account’s strategic priorities and developing appropriate value-added programs for them with a focus on holistic solution selling (i.e., packaging complementary products across business units as a holistic solution) 
 Ongoing Fulfillment and Delivery  
Accountable for the regular order-taking, fulfilment, and delivery post-conversion for assigned accounts 
Provide valuable insights on development of sales strategies to better meet customer needs prioritising a shift to holistic solution selling  
 Manage Distributor Performance 
Cultivate and build strategic relationships across a network of distributors to ensure fulfilment of customer orders 
Provide functional leadership to distributor salesforce teams in day-to-day demand generation 
Train and motivate distributor salesforce teams 
 Provide Insights on Sales Landscape and Forecast 
Provide valuable insights to sales forecasts as inputs into the overall demand planning and the Sales, Inventory and Operations Planning (SIOP) processes 
Analyse sales and industry trends and make appropriate recommendations to the KAM in an effort to ensure long-range planning needs pertaining to one’s area of responsibility are met 


Job Requirements

The ideal candidate must have a Bachelor Degree in Medical Technology, Life Science, Biomedical or related field.
At least 2 years of sales & application experience in Medical Tech and/or Laboratory equipments.
Valid driver license and own vehicle is mandatory.
The role will be based in Yogyakarta.
